---
title: "ADS 502 Project"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-04-01"
---
```{python}
#Importing Python libraries

import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import statsmodels.tools.tools as stattools
import statsmodels.api as sm
from sklearn.tree import DecisionTreeClassifier, export_graphviz
from sklearn.model_selection import train_test_split
import random
from scipy import stats
from statsmodels.stats.proportion import proportions_ztest
from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix
from sklearn.naive_bayes import MultinomialNB
from sklearn.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier
from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler
import seaborn as sns

```


```{python}
#Importing the spaceship titanic data set

train = pd.read_csv("/Users/brodr105/USD MS ADS/ADS 502/Project/spaceship-titanic/train.csv")

test = pd.read_csv("/Users/brodr105/USD MS ADS/ADS 502/Project/spaceship-titanic/test.csv")


#Keeping a raw dataset for some of EDA
raw_train = pd.read_csv("/Users/brodr105/USD MS ADS/ADS 502/Project/spaceship-titanic/train.csv")
```


```{python}
#References for attribute meaning

#PassengerId - A unique Id for each passenger. Each Id takes the form gggg_pp where gggg indicates a group the passenger is travelling with and pp is their number within the group. People in a group are often family members, but not always.

#HomePlanet - The planet the passenger departed from, typically their planet of permanent residence.

#CryoSleep - Indicates whether the passenger elected to be put into suspended animation for the duration of the voyage. Passengers in cryosleep are confined to their cabins.

#Cabin - The cabin number where the passenger is staying. Takes the form deck/num/side, where side can be either P for Port or S for Starboard.

#Destination - The planet the passenger will be debarking to.

#Age - The age of the passenger.

#VIP - Whether the passenger has paid for special VIP service during the voyage.

#RoomService - Amount the passenger has billed for room service.

#FoodCourt - Amount the passenger has billed at the food court.

#ShoppingMall - Amount the passenger has billed at the shopping mall.

#Spa - Amount the passenger has billed at the spa.

#VRDeck - Amount the passenger has billed at the VR deck

#Name - The first and last name of the passenger

#Transported - Whether the passenger was transported to another dimension. This is the target, the column you are trying to predict.


#test.csv - Personal records for the remaining one-third (~4300) of the passengers, to be used as test data. Your task is to predict the value of Transported for the passengers in this set.
```

```{python}
train.head
```


```{python}
#Number of rows and columns
train.shape
```

```{python}
train.info()
```

```{python}
train.info
```

```{python}
train['Transported'].value_counts()
```

```{python}
classdistribution = train['Transported'].value_counts(normalize=True) * 100
print(classdistribution.round(2).astype(str) + "%")
```

```{python}
train.isnull().sum()
```

```{python}
test.isnull().sum()
```

```{python}
# Train
cabin = train['Cabin'].str.split('/', expand=True)
train['Deck'] = cabin[0]
train['CabinNumber'] = pd.to_numeric(cabin[1], errors='coerce')
train['Side'] = cabin[2]

# Test
cabin_test = test['Cabin'].str.split('/', expand=True)
test['Deck'] = cabin_test[0]
test['CabinNumber'] = pd.to_numeric(cabin_test[1], errors='coerce')
test['Side'] = cabin_test[2]
```

```{python}
# Removing Cabin (extracted) and unique identifier features
train = train.drop(columns=['Cabin', 'PassengerId', 'Name'])
test  = test.drop(columns=['Cabin', 'PassengerId', 'Name'])
```

```{python}
train.head()
```

```{python}
amenities = ['RoomService', 'FoodCourt', 'ShoppingMall', 'Spa', 'VRDeck']

# Train
train[amenities] = train[amenities].fillna(0)
zeroamenities = (train[amenities] == 0).all(axis=1)
train['CryoSleep'] = train['CryoSleep'].fillna(zeroamenities.astype(bool))

# Test
test[amenities] = test[amenities].fillna(0)
zeroamenities_test = (test[amenities] == 0).all(axis=1)
test['CryoSleep'] = test['CryoSleep'].fillna(zeroamenities_test.astype(bool))
```

```{python}
amenities = ['RoomService', 'FoodCourt', 'ShoppingMall', 'Spa', 'VRDeck']

# Train
train[amenities] = train[amenities].fillna(0)
zeroamenities = (train[amenities] == 0).all(axis=1)
train['CryoSleep'] = train['CryoSleep'].fillna(zeroamenities.astype(bool))

# Test
test[amenities] = test[amenities].fillna(0)
zeroamenities_test = (test[amenities] == 0).all(axis=1)
test['CryoSleep'] = test['CryoSleep'].fillna(zeroamenities_test.astype(bool))
```

```{python}
for col in ['HomePlanet', 'Destination', 'Deck', 'Side']:
    modes = train[col].mode()[0]
    train[col] = train[col].fillna(modes)
    test[col] = test[col].fillna(modes)

# VIP - boolean mode imputation
train['VIP'] = train['VIP'].fillna(train['VIP'].mode()[0]).astype(bool)
test['VIP'] = test['VIP'].fillna(train['VIP'].mode()[0]).astype(bool)
```


```{python}
train[['Age']].plot(kind='hist', bins=40)
plt.show()
```

```{python}
train[['CabinNumber']].plot(kind='hist', bins=40)
plt.show()
```


```{python}
# Age
train['Age'] = train['Age'].fillna(train['Age'].median())
test['Age'] = test['Age'].fillna(train['Age'].median())

# Cabin number
train['CabinNumber'] = train['CabinNumber'].fillna(train['CabinNumber'].median())
test['CabinNumber'] = test['CabinNumber'].fillna(train['CabinNumber'].median())
```

```{python}
# Check nulls
train.isnull().sum()
```


```{python}
# Check nulls
test.isnull().sum()
```

```{python}
# Boolean encoding
train['CryoSleep'] = train['CryoSleep'].astype(int)
train['VIP'] = train['VIP'].astype(int)

test['CryoSleep'] = test['CryoSleep'].astype(int)
test['VIP'] = test['VIP'].astype(int)

# Encoding remaining categorical variables
categoricals = ['HomePlanet', 'Destination', 'Deck', 'Side']

train = pd.get_dummies(train, columns=categoricals)
test = pd.get_dummies(test, columns=categoricals)

train, test = train.align(test, join='left', axis=1)
```


```{python}
train.head
```

```{python}
X = train.drop(columns=['Transported'])
y = train['Transported'].astype(int)
X_test = X_test = test.drop(columns=['Transported'])
```

```{python}
# Standardizing numeric columns for modelling
numeric = ['Age', 'RoomService', 'FoodCourt', 'ShoppingMall', 'Spa', 'VRDeck', 'CabinNumber']

standardize = StandardScaler()
X[numeric] = standardize.fit_transform(X[numeric])
X_test[numeric] = standardize.transform(X_test[numeric])
```

```{python}
decisiontree = DecisionTreeClassifier(max_depth=5, random_state=42)
decisiontree.fit(X, y)

originals = pd.Series(X.columns.str.split('_').str[0], index=X.columns)

featureimportance = pd.Series(decisiontree.feature_importances_, index=X.columns)
groupedbyoriginal = featureimportance.groupby(originals).sum().sort_values(ascending=False)

plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
groupedbyoriginal.sort_values().plot(kind='barh')
plt.title("Feature Importance Scores")
plt.xlabel("Feature Importance Score")
plt.ylabel("Feature")
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
X.to_csv('X.csv', index=False)
X_test.to_csv('X_test.csv', index=False)
y.to_csv('y.csv', index=False)
```


#Brandon Bar Plot EDA


```{python}
#Note we are using the raw_train set for the EDA

#Home Planet Bar plot
counts = raw_train.groupby(['HomePlanet', 'Transported']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
counts.plot(kind='bar', width=0.7)
plt.title('HomePlanet vs Transported')
plt.xlabel('HomePlanet')
plt.ylabel('Passenger Count')
plt.legend(['Not Transported', 'Transported'])
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#Note we are using the raw_train set for the EDA

#CryoSleep
counts = raw_train.groupby(['CryoSleep', 'Transported']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
counts.plot(kind='bar', width=0.7)
plt.title('CryoSleep vs Transported')
plt.xlabel('CryoSleep')
plt.ylabel('Passenger Count')
plt.legend(['Not Transported', 'Transported'])
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#Note we are using the raw_train set for the EDA

#Destination
counts = raw_train.groupby(['Destination', 'Transported']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
counts.plot(kind='bar', width=0.7)
plt.title('Destination vs Transported')
plt.xlabel('Destination')
plt.ylabel('Passenger Count')
plt.legend(['Not Transported', 'Transported'])
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```


```{python}
#Note we are using the raw_train set for the EDA

#VIP
counts = raw_train.groupby(['VIP', 'Transported']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
counts.plot(kind='bar', width=0.7)
plt.title('VIP vs Transported')
plt.xlabel('VIP')
plt.ylabel('Passenger Count')
plt.legend(['Not Transported', 'Transported'])
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```

```{python}
#Created split cabin from partners previous code for the raw_train set
# Split Cabin
cabin = raw_train['Cabin'].str.split('/', expand=True)
raw_train['Deck'] = cabin[0]
raw_train['CabinNumber'] = pd.to_numeric(cabin[1], errors='coerce')
raw_train['Side'] = cabin[2]
```

```{python}
#Note we are using the raw_train set for the EDA

#Deck
counts = raw_train.groupby(['Deck', 'Transported']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
counts.plot(kind='bar', width=0.7)
plt.title('Deck vs Transported')
plt.xlabel('Deck')
plt.ylabel('Passenger Count')
plt.legend(['Not Transported', 'Transported'])
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

```

```{python}
#Note we are using the raw_train set for the EDA

counts = raw_train.groupby(['Side', 'Transported']).size().unstack(fill_value=0)
counts.plot(kind='bar', width=0.7)
plt.title('Side vs Transported')
plt.xlabel('Side')
plt.ylabel('Passenger Count')
plt.legend(['Not Transported', 'Transported'])
plt.xticks(rotation=30)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()
```



#Brandon Correlation Matrix

```{python}
# Combine X and y for correlation analysis
train_encoded = X.copy()
train_encoded['Transported'] = y

# Calculate correlation matrix
corr = train_encoded.corr()

# Plot heatmap
plt.figure(figsize=(18, 14))
plt.imshow(corr, cmap='coolwarm', aspect='auto')
plt.colorbar()
plt.xticks(range(len(corr.columns)), corr.columns, rotation=90)
plt.yticks(range(len(corr.columns)), corr.columns)
plt.title('Correlation Matrix of Encoded Features')
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()

# Print top correlations with Transported specifically
print("Top correlations with Transported:")
print(corr['Transported'].sort_values(ascending=False).to_string())
```
